name: Data Protection Act, No. 24 of 2019
summary: Kenya's Data Protection Act establishes a comprehensive framework for the protection of personal data, applying to all organizations that collect, process, or store personal data of individuals in Kenya. It created the Office of the Data Protection Commissioner (ODPC) as the regulatory authority.
keyRequirements: Register as a data controller or processor with the Data Commissioner
Obtain consent before collecting or processing personal data
Conduct data protection impact assessments for high-risk processing
Implement appropriate technical and organizational security measures
Notify the Data Commissioner of data breaches within 72 hours
Appoint a Data Protection Officer where required
Restrict cross-border transfers to countries with adequate protection
penalties: Administrative fines up to KES 5,000,000 or 1% of annual turnover, whichever is lower. Criminal penalties include fines up to KES 3,000,000 and/or imprisonment up to 10 years. Enforced by the Office of the Data Protection Commissioner (ODPC).
